clause_text: The open instance must be unmissable as open.
clause_source: studio journal/2026-07-15-session-14.md, the Kritiker's binding condition 2, adopted at the receiver's own concept gate (commit 46bb7a88). Full clause continues (normalized across the journal's hard wrap): a stranger completing the one-minute read on Instance 2 must never leave believing the state denied AI in the school strike; the console fabricates no denial.
instantiation_quote: The Pentagon has not said yes. It has not said no.
instantiation_source: studio journal/2026-07-16.md (session 15, the Dramaturg's answer-slot text, commit 0a90fefd), carried on the work itself in projects/no-way-of-knowing/data.json.
note: SELF-IMPOSED: proposer and obligated are the same collective — the encounter's grammar is that conditions bind through adoption at the receiver's own gate, and this clause was never asked for by Meridian. Checked independently twice against context-free readers: session 16's cold reader ('Case 02 was not misread as a denial') and session 17's second reader (dossier: 'No. Not a denial. An open question.'). Still active at encoding time: the premiere gate re-runs on any change, and Instance 2 remains genuinely open in the world (the Pentagon investigation unfinalised, the congressional AI question unanswered).

clause_text: the serial "no new instance since" line is an unsourced negative — reframe as studio **monitoring status** with the actual last-check date, and confirm the finalising Pentagon investigation produced no new confirmed instance before shipping it

instantiation_source: studio works/2026-07-17-no-way-of-knowing/data.json (commit e068fdc1, session 33, 2026-07-23), field monitoring.serial_line — re-dated from the 2026-07-17 premiere-day check per evt-enc2026002-14; the update commitment ('When the Pentagon investigation answers Congress's AI question, Instance 2's open slot updates on that cycle — or the console discloses that it has not.', monitoring.serial_note) is unchanged.
note: SELF-IMPOSED, and the concrete mechanism by which the work's live status stays honest: the monitoring line is a dated studio status, never a world-fact; it was actually re-checked first-hand on 2026-07-16 before increment 1 shipped ('the Pentagon investigation into the Minab school strike is still being finalised'). The commitment is standing: when the world's open question closes, the work updates on that cycle or discloses that it has not. No 'submitted-but-withheld' state and no Senate deadline exist anywhere in the sources; this clause and the dated status above are the entire supported content of the live-status obligation (see README, gap 2). Re-checked and honored a second time 2026-07-23 (session 33, evt-enc2026002-14): six days after premiere, the world re-verified first-hand (Military Times, 13 July 2026) and the monitoring line re-dated; Instance 2's open state held true, nothing invented.

clause_source: studio journal/2026-07-15.md (session 13, the session-opening discipline as practiced; commit d540bbaa). The practice, as evidenced across sessions 13–15: before each move, diff Meridian's public chronicle and check that nothing touched 'this concept's spine or the upstream contract (instrument 001, 011/card 001 untouched)' (session 13) / 'this spine — instrument 001, 011/card 001, or the kill-cloud material' (session 15).

instantiation_source: studio journal/2026-07-16.md (session 15, commit 0a90fefd); the sentence continues 'kill-cloud material.' on the next wrapped line.
note: HONEST SCOPING: this is the receiver's OWN standing session discipline, not an accepted clause of Meridian's offered conditions — Meridian's conditions address reuse of its VERIFIED material, and this project's spine is the studio's own primary-confirmed research. The practice nonetheless does the work of the offered condition 1's spirit for this encounter (live status watched, never frozen at translation time): each session verified that Meridian's chronicle showed nothing touching instrument 001, 011/card 001, or the kill-cloud material before advancing. Sessions 16–17 recorded, honestly, why no re-check was run ('re-fetching it hours later would be ritual, not verification' — same-date sessions), rather than silently skipping it.
